hypothesis: Fine-tuning diverse pre-trained molecular and materials foundation models on a shared calibration set of ≤1,000 representative OMol25 out-of-distribution structures will reduce their average pairwise CKNNA on a held-out OMol25 test set and increase the correlation between alignment and OOD energy-prediction accuracy, indicating decreased spurious OOD convergence and improved task-specific representational specificity.
